_comment: Drug page generation queue. Each entry produces ONE JSON file under content/data/drugs/<slug>.json via the coveredusa-drug-writer agent. Status: 'queued' (ready), 'writing' (in flight), 'verified' (passed verifier), 'flagged' (verifier flagged), 'write_failed' (writer crashed - retry-eligible).

drugs.name: Ozempic
drugs.notes: Type 2 diabetes + weight management. Massive recent demand. Pen-injector pricing. Verify 2026 Medicare rates + Novo Nordisk PAP. Wegovy is same molecule for weight loss (different label).

drugs.name: Metformin
drugs.notes: First-line type 2 diabetes drug. Generic, very cheap retail ($4-$15/month). Strong angle: low retail cost vs hospital inpatient markup. No J-code (oral only).

drugs.name: Humira
drugs.notes: Most-prescribed biologic. $6,000+/month retail. Biosimilars now available. AbbVie PAP and Humira Complete program. Verify 2026 Part D coverage.

drugs.name: Eliquis
drugs.drugClass: Direct oral anticoagulant (DOAC, factor Xa inhibitor)
drugs.notes: IRA Medicare drug price negotiation: Eliquis is one of the first 10 drugs with negotiated price effective 2026-01-01. Must reference the negotiation and the new MFP (Maximum Fair Price). Bristol Myers Squibb + Pfizer co-market. PAP: Eliquis 360 Support.

drugs.name: Atorvastatin
drugs.notes: Generic statin, very cheap. $4-$15/month retail. High-volume prescription. Brand Lipitor mostly displaced by generic. No specific Part D cap.

drugs.name: Lisinopril
drugs.notes: First-line hypertension. Generic. $4-$10/month retail. Wide use, sometimes combined with HCTZ.

drugs.name: Albuterol
drugs.notes: Rescue inhaler for asthma/COPD. $30-$80 retail per inhaler. Generic available but brand still common. Pediatric and adult versions. AB-rated generics.

drugs.name: Jardiance
drugs.notes: IRA Medicare drug price negotiation: Jardiance is one of the first 10 negotiated drugs with MFP effective 2026-01-01. Type 2 diabetes + heart failure + CKD indication. Boehringer Ingelheim. PAP: BI Cares Foundation.

drugs.name: Levothyroxine
drugs.notes: Hypothyroidism. Lifelong therapy for most patients. Very cheap generic ($4-$15/month). Bioequivalence concerns between manufacturers — sometimes patients are non-substitutable. Common refill confusion.

drugs.name: Wegovy
drugs.notes: Same molecule as Ozempic but FDA-approved specifically for chronic weight management. Higher dose. $1,300+/month retail. Most insurance plans don't cover weight-loss drugs (Medicare excludes). PAP eligibility limited. Strong consumer demand.
